title: CaseSpec (bad-case suite entry)
description: Portable shape for eval/bad_cases/<id>.yaml entries. closure_criterion follows Constitution §1.7-B (positive shape + anti-pattern + anchor phrases — human-judgment paragraph, NOT keyword match). Joint authoring per Constitution §5 state ledgers: Deliver curates structure; human authors closure_criterion.

source_suite.description: Suite role. shadow_holdout MUST NOT be read by Dev sandbox (process/delivery-loop.md §4.2.8 anti-pattern #4).

input.description: Free-shape input the system under test consumes. Type A: customer message + state; Type B: SOP step + slot values; Type C: demo trigger.

expected.description: Free-shape expected behavior summary. NOT the closure_criterion (which is the human-judgment paragraph). This field is for quick-glance reference; closure_criterion is the contract.

judge.description: deterministic_assertion only for tier1_smoke; semantic decisions go through judge_llm or judge_rubric (Constitution §1.5).

primitives.description: 6-primitive frozen trace_check DSL per process/typeA-runtime-architecture-skeleton.md Δ-6. 4 data primitives (tool_call_present / tool_call_order / slot_collected / session_flag) + 2 combinators (any_of / all_of). The grammar is intentionally minimal and rejects keyword/regex/message-content match at parse time (Constitution §1.7-B structural defence). Adopters with domain-specific trace surfaces add Tier-3 extensions; the 6 here are the portable Tier-2 surface.

anchor_phrases.description: Anchor phrases are EVIDENCE the judge cites, NOT a passing condition (Constitution §1.7-B).

failure_brief_ref.description: Path to docs/diagnostics/failure-briefs/<id>.md this case was promoted from (if applicable).
